input.title: Pharmacy Dispensing and Inventory Control System
input.description: Pharmacy Dispensing and Inventory Control System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Java Spring Boot, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Prescription Intake and Validation
requirements: The system must support prescription intake and validation as a complete business workflow within the application.
Prescription Intake and Validation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with prescription intake and validation from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to prescription intake and validation for operational support.

FR-02.title: Patient Medication Profile Lookup
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end patient medication profile lookup capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Patient Medication Profile Lookup should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to patient medication profile lookup without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within patient medication profile lookup and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-03.title: Drug Interaction Alerting
requirements: The system must trigger drug interaction alerting when the relevant business events or threshold conditions occur.
Users should be able to configure delivery channels, timing, or opt-in preferences related to drug interaction alerting.
Drug Interaction Alerting must record delivery outcomes, retries, and failures for operational follow-up.
The interface should let authorized users review recent drug interaction alerting events and the current notification status.

FR-04.title: Controlled Substance Verification
requirements: Users must be able to complete controlled substance verification through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing controlled substance verification to continue.
Controlled Substance Verification must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for controlled substance verification should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-05.title: Dispensing Queue Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end dispensing queue management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Dispensing Queue Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to dispensing queue management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within dispensing queue management and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-07.title: Batch and Expiry Tracking
requirements: The system must capture batch and expiry tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Batch and Expiry Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile batch and expiry tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during batch and expiry tracking.

The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving inventory reorder thresholds changes.
Inventory Reorder Thresholds must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for inventory reorder thresholds.

FR-01.title: Plan Prescription Intake and Validation Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for prescription intake and validation and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: The system must support prescription intake and validation as a complete business workflow within the application; Prescription Intake and Validation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with prescription intake and validation from the relevant module.

Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for prescription intake and validation are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for prescription intake and validation can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.description: Build the backend services for prescription intake and validation on React, Java Spring Boot, PostgreSQL.
